advancement.productivefrogs.predator_bre: Breed two frog species into a predator frog - it hunts mobs, not slimes
advancement.productivefrogs.apex_bred.de: Cross two predators into an Apex Frog - the only frog that can power a boss altar
advancement.productivefrogs.mob_farmed.d: Watch a predator frog devour a mob and collect its drops
Complete a boss altar summon with an Apex Frog installed
advancement.productivefrogs.root.title: Productive Frogs
advancement.productivefrogs.root.descrip: Capture a Resource Slime in a bucket, or bottle some frogspawn
advancement.productivefrogs.farm_cave.de: Obtain a Cave Froglight - your Cave frogs are farming ores
advancement.productivefrogs.farm_geode.d: Obtain a Geode Froglight - your Geode frogs are farming gems
advancement.productivefrogs.farm_bog.des: Obtain a Bog Froglight - your Bog frogs are farming the swamp
advancement.productivefrogs.farm_tide.de: Obtain a Tide Froglight - your Tide frogs are farming the deep
advancement.productivefrogs.farm_inferna: Obtain an Infernal Froglight - your Infernal frogs are farming the Nether
advancement.productivefrogs.farm_void.de: Obtain a Void Froglight - your Void frogs are farming the End
advancement.productivefrogs.machines.des: Craft the Slime Milker, Froglight Crucible, and Casting Mold
productivefrogs.princess_kiss.hint: Right-click a frog to turn it into a villager
productivefrogs.frog_net.empty: Right-click a frog to catch it
tooltip.productivefrogs.catalyst.apply: Drop into a Slime Milk source to apply
tooltip.productivefrogs.catalyst.count: More slime spawns before it runs dry
item.productivefrogs.vt_upgrade_bounty.t: Adds to the loaded frog's Bounty, so it makes more per cycle.
item.productivefrogs.vt_upgrade_appetite: Adds to the loaded frog's Appetite, shortening each cycle.
Auto-smelts each output, so you get the smelted item instead of a raw Froglight. Cannot pair with a Melter.
item.productivefrogs.vt_upgrade_melter.t: Melts each Froglight into molten fluid, piped out the bottom. Cannot pair with a Smelter.
item.productivefrogs.vt_upgrade_overcloc: Runs the whole block 50% faster. Draws RF; stalls with no power. Stacks up to three.
message.productivefrogs.terrarium.not_so: Shell needs a solid block here.
message.productivefrogs.terrarium.machin: A machine block can't sit on a corner or edge - faces only.
This machine must face into the cavity.
message.productivefrogs.terrarium.sprink: Sprinklers go in the ceiling only.
message.productivefrogs.terrarium.no_inc: The Terrarium needs at least one Incubator.
productivefrogs.gui.incubator.empty_hint: Right-click with a bottle of frog eggs
productivefrogs.gui.incubator.sweetslime: Right-click with a Sweetslime to speed up
productivefrogs.jei.variant_slime.info: A %1$s. Killed by a %2$s, drops a Froglight stamped with this variant. Use the Slime Milker to convert into a Slime Milk bucket for infinite renewable production.
productivefrogs.jei.variant_froglight.in: Dropped when a %1$s eats a %2$s. Smelts in a furnace to the resource it represents.
productivefrogs.jei.frog.info: Eats %1$s variants. When fed a variant Resource Slime (e.g. an Iron Slime), drops a Froglight stamped with that variant. Category-only slimes are intermediates and produce nothing.
productivefrogs.jei.tadpole.info: Matures into a %1$s while in water.
productivefrogs.jei.midas_frog.info: Midas eats Mimic Slimes (the Equivalence lane) and drops a Prismatic Froglight stamped with the slime's item. Prime frogspawn with a Princess's Kiss to make one; Midas breeds true and its Bounty scales the drop.
productivefrogs.jei.midas_tadpole.info: Matures into Midas while in water.
productivefrogs.jei.frog_egg.info: Place on water to spawn a Primed Frog Egg block. After a delay, hatches into 1-3 Tadpoles that mature into %1$s.
productivefrogs.jei.primed_egg.info: Hatches after a delay into 1-3 Tadpoles that mature into %1$s. Must be placed on a water source.
productivefrogs.jei.slime_milk.info: Place in the world to create a Slime Milk source block. Periodically spawns a %1$s on a solid adjacent block. The output of feeding a variant Slime Bucket into the Slime Milker.
productivefrogs.jei.parent_species.bog.i: Spawns in swamps and mangrove swamps. Infuse with a variant primer (clay, mud, moss, dirt, leather, etc.) to convert into a variant Resource Slime that frogs can eat.
Spawns in dripstone caves, deep dark, and lush caves. Infuse with a variant primer (iron ingot, copper ingot, gold ingot, redstone, coal, etc.) to convert into a variant Resource Slime.
productivefrogs.jei.parent_species.geode: Spawns in mountain peaks (stony, jagged, frozen) and snowy slopes. Infuse with a gem primer (amethyst shard, diamond, emerald, lapis lazuli, calcite, or tuff) to convert into a variant Resource Slime.
Spawns in oceans (deep, warm, lukewarm). Infuse with a prismarine shard or sponge to convert into a variant Resource Slime.
productivefrogs.jei.parent_species.infer: Spawns in the Nether (nether wastes, basalt deltas, soul sand valley). Infuse with magma cream to convert into a variant Resource Slime.
Spawns in the End (outer islands). Infuse with an ender pearl to convert into a variant Resource Slime.
productivefrogs.jei.empty_frog_egg.info: Bottle a vanilla frogspawn block (right-click with a glass bottle) to fill this. Prime the resulting filled bottle with a variant primer item to assign a species.
productivefrogs.jei.slime_milker.info: Furnace-style production block. Right-click to open. Place a Slime Bucket containing a variant Resource Slime in the input slot — after 100 ticks, it converts to the matching Slime Milk bucket. Hopper-compatible.
productivefrogs.jei.slime_churn.info: The Milker's inverse. Load a Slime Milk bucket and empty buckets: the Churn spawns that variant's slimes straight into the buckets, on the same schedule and budget as placed Slime Milk - catalysts included. The spent milk container returns in the lower output slot. Hopper-compatible.
productivefrogs.jei.slurry_press.info: Presses a netted mob into a Bucket of Mob Slurry. Catch a mob with a Frog Net (or an Ender Net for teleporters), load it with an empty bucket, and the press does the rest. Bosses and Resource Slimes refuse to be pressed. Hopper-compatible.
productivefrogs.jei.mob_slurry_basin.inf: Pour a Bucket of Mob Slurry in and the basin spawns that mob on a budget, like placed Slime Milk. Toss milk catalysts in to buff it. Waterloggable; teleporting mobs spawned here cannot teleport away.
productivefrogs.jei.slime_milk_basin.inf: Pour a Slime Milk bucket in and the basin spawns that variant's Resource Slimes on the standard milk budget - catalysts included. A half-block, waterloggable alternative to placing the milk as a source block.
productivefrogs.jei.ender_net.info: A Frog Net reinforced with an ender pearl: catches teleporting mobs (endermen, shulkers) and carries Apex Frogs to their boss altars. Bosses cannot be netted.
productivefrogs.jei.liquid_experience.in: Experience as a fluid (20 mB per XP point). Boss altars bank their XP payout as Liquid Experience - pipe it out of the Hatch from any face. Interoperable with other mods' XP fluids via the c:experience tag.
productivefrogs.jei.boss_altar_hatch.inf: The altar's brain and output chest. Build the altar around it, install its matching Apex Frog (click with a filled net), load the receptacles, and the summon runs on its own - raw boss drops land here, XP banks as Liquid Experience.
productivefrogs.jei.terrarium_controller: The heart of a Terrarium: a sealed 5x5x5 frog habitat. Build a one-block-thick shell (any solid blocks) around a 5x5x5 cavity, with this Controller and exactly one Hatch set in the faces (not corners or edges), each facing inward. Add Incubators (optional) to breed frogs in place, or lead caught frogs in yourself. Feed it Slime Milk by hand or by pipe; it funnels one variant at a time out to the ceiling Sprinklers. Right-click to see whether it's formed and why not.
productivefrogs.jei.sprinkler.info: Sits in the Terrarium ceiling (up to 25). The Controller fills it with one bucket of Slime Milk, and it rains that variant's slimes down into the cavity on the exact placed-source schedule - catalysts and all. Right-click with an empty bucket to drain it back out. It pauses when the cavity is full.
productivefrogs.jei.incubator.info: Seeds and grows frogs inside a Terrarium. Right-click with a Frog Egg to start one; it matures and releases the frog into the cavity with its stats intact. Frogs bred inside a formed Terrarium lay into the nearest Incubator instead of seeking water. At the frog cap it holds the matured frog until there's room.
productivefrogs.jei.hatch.info: The Terrarium's output. Inside a formed Terrarium, every Froglight a frog produces drops straight into this 18-slot inventory - no item entities. Pipe them out, or right-click to open it. When it's full the frogs stop eating until you clear space.
productivefrogs.jei.spawnery.info: Turns glass bottles into bottled frogspawn, fueled by slime balls (one ball per bottle). A primer is required: a slime ball primes plain vanilla frogspawn, or a species primer (iron ingot for Cave, amethyst shard for Geode, clay ball for Bog, prismarine shard for Tide, blaze powder for Infernal, an ender pearl for Void) primes that species' eggs. Off by default; modpacks can retune the species primers.
productivefrogs.jei.count_catalyst.info: Toss into a placed Slime Milk source to extend it: adds spawns to the source's remaining count. Stack as many as you like - count is uncapped. The buff is saved to the source and survives re-bucketing.
productivefrogs.jei.speed_catalyst.info: Toss into a placed Slime Milk source to speed it up: each one shortens the time between slime spawns, up to a cap. The buff is saved to the source and survives re-bucketing.
productivefrogs.jei.quantity_catalyst.in: Toss into a placed Slime Milk source to widen each spawn: each one adds another slime per spawn, up to a cap. The buff is saved to the source and survives re-bucketing.
productivefrogs.jei.infinite_catalyst.in: Toss into a placed Slime Milk source to make it never run dry. Built from Count catalysts - the final tier of the count line. Saved to the source and survives re-bucketing.
productivefrogs.jade.boss_altar.no_frog: %s: waiting for its Apex Frog (sneak + filled Frog Net)
productivefrogs.jei.empty_slime_bucket.i: Right-click a size-1 Resource Slime to capture it. The bucket records the slime's variant (or species, for category-only slimes) and can be released elsewhere or fed into the Slime Milker.
productivefrogs.jei.empty_tadpole_bucket: Right-click a Resource Tadpole to capture it. The bucket records the tadpole's species and can be released elsewhere — released tadpoles will continue maturing into a Resource Frog.
productivefrogs.ender_net.empty: Right-click any mob to catch it.
productivefrogs.ender_net.release: Right-click a block to release, or press it into Mob Slurry.
productivefrogs.mob_slurry_bucket.pour: Pour into a Mob Slurry Basin to respawn the mob.
book.productivefrogs.guide.name: Productive Frogs
book.productivefrogs.guide.tooltip: The in-game guide to Productive Frogs.
book.productivefrogs.guide.getting_start: Every resource here comes from one loop instead of a pickaxe. Each Resource Slime is tied to one resource. Its matching Frog eats the slime and drops a Froglight, and smelting that Froglight gives you the resource. Set the chain up once and it runs on its own.

A Froglight is what a Frog drops when it eats its matching slime. Smelt it for a copy of the resource. To gather them without standing around, run the loop inside a Terrarium, or pin a Frog on a Sweetslimed Lily Pad so its drops always land in the same place.
book.productivefrogs.guide.terrarium.ter: The Terrarium is a sealed multiblock that runs the whole loop on its own. Seal the shell, then pipe Slime Milk into the Controller. Sprinklers in the ceiling rain slimes down, your frogs eat them, and the Hatch collects the Froglights for you to pull out.

The shell is 7 wide, 7 deep, and 6 tall around a 5 by 5 by 4 cavity. Any full block works, glass included so you can watch. Place the Controller and look at it: it lights up when the structure is valid. Sprinklers mount on the ceiling; the Incubator and Hatch sit on the walls.
book.productivefrogs.guide.appliances.vi: The Virtual Terrarium runs one frog's whole loop inside two blocks, with no live slimes or frogs to render. Stack the glass Dome on the Processor to form it, drop a netted frog into the frog slot, and pipe its feedstock into the tank: Slime Milk for a Resource frog, Mimic Milk for a Midas, Mob Slurry for a Predator. It eats on its own and drops the results out the bottom. One frog runs at the normal rate, so a full built Terrarium still out-produces it, but this costs no space and no upkeep.

Four upgrade slots take dedicated upgrades that stack: Bounty and Appetite push the frog's stats, Capacity and Everflow stretch the feedstock. A Smelter turns each Froglight straight into its smelted item; a Melter turns it into molten fluid out the bottom, and the two can't both be installed. Smelter, Melter, and the Overclock (50% faster) draw RF, and stall if the buffer runs dry.
book.productivefrogs.guide.appliances.sl: The Slime Milker turns a captured slime into Slime Milk without placing a source block and catching a spawn by hand. Put a slime bucket in the input slot and after a short cook it hands back a Slime Milk bucket tagged with that slime's resource. The milk remembers which slime it came from, so it keeps that resource through tanks and pipes. Optional station, on by default; a pack can switch it off.

One bucket of Slime Milk, tagged with the resource it came from. Place it as a source to spawn that resource's slimes, run it through the Slime Churn for pre-bucketed slimes, or pipe it wherever the resource needs to go.

The Slime Churn is the Milker run backwards. Load a Slime Milk bucket plus a stack of empty buckets and it produces captured Slime Buckets of that resource, on the exact spawn rules a placed milk source uses: the same timing and the same number of slimes per bucket, catalysts included. Nothing spawns in the world, so there is no slime to chase and no crowding limit to bump into.

A milk bucket only makes so many slimes before it runs dry, and its tooltip shows how many are left; a half-spent bucket can be pulled out and placed in the world to finish there. Empty buckets are the capture containers, one used per slime, and the spent milk container leaves by its own slot so a hopper draining slime buckets never grabs the empty. If it runs out of empty buckets or the output backs up, it stalls with none of the count wasted and picks up where it paused once the blockage clears.
book.productivefrogs.guide.appliances.sp: The Spawnery bottles frogspawn so the frog side of the loop can start in a world with no swamp or mangrove. It is config-gated and off by default; a normal world has vanilla frogspawn and never needs it. A pack that wants a skyblock or no-frog start turns it on.

Feed it glass bottles, slime balls for fuel at one ball per bottle, and a primer. The primer is required and it decides the egg: a slime ball primes plain vanilla frogspawn, while a species primer item primes that species' egg, iron for Cave, amethyst for Geode, clay for Bog, prismarine for Tide, blaze powder for Infernal, an ender pearl for Void. The egg is species-level, not resource-level, and a pack can retune which item primes each species.
book.productivefrogs.guide.appliances.cr: The Crucible is a heated basin that melts a Froglight into fluid, a third way to cash one out beside smelting and crushing. Right-click a Froglight in one at a time, the way you feed a composter; there is no GUI, so look at the block with Jade to read melt progress, tank contents, and current heat. Heat comes from the block directly below, and the hotter it is the faster the melt: a torch works from day one, lava is the reliable mid-tier, and fire on netherrack runs hottest. No heat below means no melting at all.

A metal Froglight melts down to two ingots' worth of molten metal, so it doubles what a Froglight is worth over smelting one. A Water or Lava Froglight gives a full bucket of water or lava, renewable through the frog loop. The Crucible also melts raw cobblestone, stone, gravel, or netherrack straight to lava with heat below. The tank holds one fluid at a time, so drain it before switching to a different melt.

Molten metal is not an ingot yet. The Casting Mold turns it back into ingots, and unlike the Crucible it has a screen with a fluid gauge and an output slot. Set a Mold directly on top of a Crucible and it pulls molten straight from the tank below with no pipes: heat source, Crucible, Mold, a three-block stack that runs the whole melt-and-cast loop in a plain pack. Optional lane, on by default.
book.productivefrogs.guide.appliances.sl: The Slurry Press empties a captured mob into a bucket. Put a filled Ender Net and an empty bucket in and it presses out a Mob Slurry bucket carrying that mob, handing the net back empty. Boss mobs are refused here, the same as when you try to net one. Where Mob Slurry goes next is covered in the Predation chapter. Optional station, on by default.
A bucket holding one captured mob as fluid. It is the portable form of a caught mob; the Predation chapter covers what you pour it into.
book.productivefrogs.guide.boss_altars.a: Apex frogs are the highest frog tier. You breed them from set predator-frog crosses, and each Apex is tied to one boss: the Wither, the Ender Dragon, the Warden, or the Elder Guardian. An Apex does not hunt in the world the way a Predator does. Its one job is to run its boss altar.

Catch the matching Apex in a net, then shift-right-click the altar's hatch to install it. An armed altar summons its boss on a timer and the Apex eats a harmless replica, so you never fight the real thing: no boss bar, no attacks, no broken blocks. The raw kill drops land in the hatch chest and the experience banks as Liquid Experience in the hatch, ready to pipe out. The whole boss tier sits behind one config switch; with it off the altar blocks cannot be crafted and no summon runs.
book.productivefrogs.guide.boss_altars.w: The Wither altar stands 5 by 5 by 5. Build it around the Withered Star, mount the hatch on the wall, then load the receptacles and install the Wither Apex.

You cannot build this altar until you have a Withered Star, and that is crafted from a Nether Star, so you have to kill a Wither the normal way at least once first. Install the Wither Apex Frog on the hatch and load the receptacles with a full Wither summon: four soul sand and three wither skeleton skulls, by hand or piped in. Each run raises a harmless Wither for the frog to eat, then drops a Nether Star into the hatch and banks its experience as Liquid Experience.
book.productivefrogs.guide.boss_altars.d: The Dragon altar is built on the End exit portal, 7 wide and 10 tall. Set an End Crystal in each receptacle and install the Dragon Apex on the hatch.

This altar is built around the End exit portal, and you cannot make it until you have a Dragon Egg, which only drops from the first, real dragon kill. Install the Dragon Apex Frog on the hatch and drop an End Crystal into each of the four receptacles. Each run grows a harmless dragon for the frog to eat and pays into the hatch: Dragon's Breath every time, a fresh Dragon Egg (this is on by default, so the egg is renewable here), and a Princess's Kiss. The experience banks as Liquid Experience.
book.productivefrogs.guide.boss_altars.w: The Warden altar is a sealed 5 by 5 by 5 pit around the Echoing Catalyst. Load one Sculk Shrieker into each receptacle and install the Warden Apex on the hatch.

A sealed deep-dark pit. You cannot build it until you have an Echoing Catalyst, crafted from a Sculk Catalyst. Install the Warden Apex Frog on the hatch and load the four receptacles with one Sculk Shrieker each, the four shriekers a Warden needs to appear. Each run raises a harmless Warden, no boss bar and no sonic boom, then drops a Sculk Catalyst and an Echo Shard into the hatch and banks the experience as Liquid Experience. It is the only renewable source of Echo Shards in the game.
book.productivefrogs.guide.boss_altars.e: The Elder Guardian altar is a sealed 5 by 5 by 5 water tank around the Monument Core. Keep the inside flooded, load a Prismarine Crystal into each receptacle, and install the Elder Apex on the hatch.

A sealed 5 by 5 by 5 water tank; the whole inside has to stay flooded or the altar stops running. You cannot build it until you have a Monument Core, crafted from a Wet Sponge. Install the Elder Apex Frog on the hatch and load the four receptacles with one Prismarine Crystal each. Each run swells a harmless Elder Guardian, with no mining fatigue to fight through, then pays a Wet Sponge and the guardian's other drops into the hatch, and banks the experience as Liquid Experience.
book.productivefrogs.guide.equivalence.o: The Equivalence lane copies items the frogs can't make. If an item has no slime of its own, vanilla or modded, this lane is how you duplicate it. The whole lane is optional and off by default. Set equivalence to true in the config to switch it on. It runs on power, next to the normal six-species machines, and never changes how they work.

The Alembic turns an item into a Mimic Slime. Milk that slime into Mimic Milk and place it to spawn more Mimic Slimes, the same way slime milk multiplies. A Midas frog eats the slimes and drops a Prismatic Froglight for each one. The Distiller reads the Froglight and gives the original item back.
book.productivefrogs.guide.equivalence.a: The Alembic runs on power. Put an item and an empty bucket in it, feed it power, and it fills the bucket with a Mimic Slime carrying that item. You supply the bucket, so the lane never eats your buckets. It only takes plain items: anything with saved data, like a written book or an enchanted tool, is refused, along with items the frogs already make and the machine's own outputs. The arrow shows a red X when the item in the slot can't be copied.
The Alembic is copper, glass, and a nether star, so you need to have beaten the Wither before you can craft it. The Distiller costs the same.
book.productivefrogs.guide.equivalence.m: A Mimic Slime is its own creature, separate from the resource slimes. It stays small and never splits. The Alembic hands you one already in a bucket; place it and it acts like any slime. Milk it in a Milker to get Mimic Milk, then place the milk to spawn more Mimic Slimes for the frog to eat.

Mimic Milk carries the copied item on the fluid itself. PF's own Milker and Churn read it fine, but piping it through a third-party fluid tank can strip the item id. Keep the milk in Productive Frogs gear and it stays intact.

Midas is a frog kind of its own, made by right-clicking frogspawn with a Princess's Kiss. It eats only Mimic Slimes and ignores every resource slime. Each slime it eats drops a Prismatic Froglight carrying that slime's item, and a higher Bounty stat means more Froglights per meal. Two Midas frogs breed into more Midas frogs.
The Ender Dragon drops the Princess's Kiss. Right-click frogspawn with it to prime a Midas egg. Because the Kiss comes from the dragon, the whole lane sits behind that fight.
book.productivefrogs.guide.equivalence.d: The Distiller is the far end of the lane. Feed it a Prismatic Froglight and power and it gives back the item that Froglight was made from, read straight off the Froglight. It is not a smelting recipe, so it always returns exactly what went in.

A Prismatic Froglight isn't a separate item, it's a normal Froglight carrying an item instead of a resource. Its name and color come from whatever the Midas frog ate. The Distiller is the only thing that turns it back.
book.productivefrogs.guide.froglights.fr: A Froglight is what a frog drops when it eats its matching slime. It carries the same resource the slime did, so smelting one gives back a copy of that resource. It is also a block in its own right: place it and it stays lit at full brightness, light level 15.

Frogs drop Froglights wherever they happen to eat, which is a nuisance to chase down. Run the loop inside a Terrarium and its Hatch gathers the drops for you. Or perch a frog on a Sweetslimed Lily Pad so it stays put and everything lands in one spot.
book.productivefrogs.guide.froglights.br: If a slime is carrying a potion effect when a frog eats it, the Froglight it drops is stamped with that effect. Place the Froglight and it gives off an aura of the effect to nearby players; hold it and you get the effect yourself. One effect you can brew this way is the Potion of Hopping, which throws you forward when you jump. This is optional and on by default; you can turn it off in the config.
book.productivefrogs.guide.frogs.species: Every frog belongs to one of six species: Cave, Geode, Bog, Tide, Infernal, and Void. There is one frog entity, and the species is its identity. Each species is named for a slime category and eats only that category's Resource Slime, dropping the matching resource's Froglight. A Cave Frog eats Cave slimes and ignores every other kind. Breed two of the same species and the tadpoles come out that species. You get your first frog by priming frogspawn into a species with the Frog Egg item or a Primed Frog Egg block, or from a spawn egg.

A Frog Egg is bottled frogspawn that carries a species. Combine a glass bottle with frogspawn to fill it, and if that spawn is a set species the egg keeps it. Use it to store and move a frog line without hauling live tadpoles around.
book.productivefrogs.guide.frogs.stats_a: Every frog carries three stats that breeding can raise. Appetite sets how much it eats and how fast. Bounty sets how much it drops; on a predator frog it is the looting level of the kill. Reach sets how far away it will target prey. Feed two frogs Sweetslime to put them in love mode and they lay tadpoles. The stats pass down from conception to egg to tadpole to frog, and each breeding has a chance to nudge one stat up. Two of the same kind breed the same kind; four specific resource-species crosses instead produce predator frogs, covered in the Predation chapter. Point Jade at a frog to read its stats.

Sweetslime is the breeding treat. Feed it to two frogs standing near each other to start them breeding, the way wheat works for cows. It is the only thing that breeds frogs, so keep a stack on hand while you are building up a stat line.
book.productivefrogs.guide.frogs.frog_ne: The Frog Net picks up a frog into an item. Right-click a frog to catch it; right-click the ground to set it back down. The filled net holds the whole frog, stats and species included, so moving a bred frog across your base or into a new pen loses nothing. It only catches frogs. For any other mob, see the Ender Net in the Predation chapter. The net is optional and on by default; a config toggle turns it off.
book.productivefrogs.guide.frogs.lily_pa: A Sweetslimed Lily Pad is a perch. Stand a frog on one and it stops wandering and stays where you put it. Because the frog holds its spot, every Froglight it drops lands in the same place, so a hopper or a single chest underneath catches the whole output. It is optional and on by default; a config toggle turns it off.
book.productivefrogs.guide.optional_extr: Kill a small Resource Slime with the Cleaver and it drops that slime's Froglight, the same one a frog would make from eating it. If the slime was brewed, the potion effect carries onto the Froglight too. Looting raises the count. Bigger slimes just split when hit, so cut them down first. This is the hands-on version of the frog loop: wade into a slime farm and swing instead of waiting on frogs.

The Cleaver is netherite-tier but hits harder: 12 attack damage, fire-resistant, and it takes the usual sword enchants. Its recipe calls for a Nether Star, a Dragon Egg, and Dragon's Breath, so you can only build it once you have beaten both the Wither and the Ender Dragon. This feature is optional and on by default. A pack can switch it off, which drops the recipe from crafting, JEI, and the creative tab.

Kill any frog and it drops 1 to 2 Raw Frog Legs, more with Looting. This covers every kind of frog: vanilla ones, Resource Frogs, and frogs added by other mods. Tadpoles are not frogs and drop nothing. If the frog was on fire when it died, it drops Cooked Frog Legs straight away, the same way a burning cow drops steak.

Cook Raw Frog Legs in a furnace, smoker, or over a campfire; the smoker is fastest. Raw is chicken-tier food and fills little; cooked fills about as much as cooked chicken. On a skyblock where animals are scarce, this makes frogs a renewable meat source, though you have to kill a producer to get it.

One tier up from cooked: it fills about as much as rabbit stew. Craft it shapeless from a bowl, Cooked Frog Legs, a brown mushroom, and a red mushroom. It stacks to one and hands the empty bowl back when eaten. The whole Frog Legs feature is optional and on by default; switching it off stops the drops and hides the items and recipes.

The Ender Dragon drops one Princess's Kiss when you kill it. Right-click any frog with it and, over about ten seconds of hearts, the frog turns into a plain villager. The villager is unemployed and free to take a profession, never a nitwit, and a named frog passes its name along. One Kiss is enough to start a villager economy, since you can breed from there. The dragon is the price.

The Kiss has a second job in the Equivalence lane: use it on a frog to prime it into a Midas frog, the frog that turns Mimic Slimes into copies of other items. Both uses are optional and on by default, though the Equivalence lane itself is a separate switch that ships off. Turning the Kiss off stops the dragon drop and hides the item.

Most of this mod's features can be switched on or off one at a time in the config. A toggle gates crafting, JEI, and the creative tab: turn a feature off and its recipes and items stop showing up. It does not touch the world. A block you already placed keeps working, so disabling a feature partway through a save is safe.

The features you can toggle include predators, the boss altars, the Equivalence lane, the Spawnery, milk catalysts, Frog Legs, the Froglight Cleaver, Princess's Kiss, the lily pad perch, and brewed Froglights. Most ship on. The Equivalence lane and the Spawnery ship off, since they shift the pack's balance the most.
book.productivefrogs.guide.predation.pre: A Predator Frog hunts vanilla mobs the way a Resource Frog hunts slimes. When it eats one, the drops are credited to you. There are four kinds, and each one hunts a single realm: the Prowler takes Overworld mobs, the Cinder the Nether, the Gulper aquatic mobs, and the Rift the End. A predator ignores mobs outside its realm.

You do not find Predator Frogs in the wild. Each kind hatches from a set cross of two resource species, and same-kind predators breed true after that. What a predator will eat comes from a preset list of prey, kept separate from what vanilla frogs eat, so ordinary frogs leave those mobs alone.
book.productivefrogs.guide.predation.how: When a predator eats a mob, the game counts the kill as yours. An unseen stand-in for you swings a Looting sword, so the mob dies the same death it would if you had killed it: you get the player-only drops, the Looting bonus, and the XP, none of it faked. The frog's Bounty sets how much Looting the sword swings with, up to Looting III at the top of the stat. Higher Bounty, better drops. Loot falls on the ground for a hopper to pick up, and the XP comes out as orbs.
book.productivefrogs.guide.predation.cap: Natural spawns feed a predator on their own, but you can farm a mob on purpose. The Ender Net catches any living mob whole, stats and all. Put a filled net and an empty bucket into the Slurry Press and it presses out a Mob Slurry bucket. Bosses are refused: the Press will not take a net holding one.

Pour a Mob Slurry bucket into a Basin and it respawns that mob for a predator to eat, over and over. Mobs spawned this way cannot teleport, so endermen and shulkers stay where the Basin puts them. Waterlog the Basin for aquatic mobs.
book.productivefrogs.guide.predation.liq: Liquid Experience is XP in fluid form. A full bucket is worth exactly 50 points, and drinking one gives you all 50. The boss altars pay it out alongside their drops, so a boss kill can fill a tank instead of scattering orbs.
book.productivefrogs.guide.slimes_and_mi: Every Resource Slime carries a single resource. Feed one to a frog of the same category and the frog turns it into a Froglight you can smelt. A frog only eats slimes of its own category, so keep your species sorted. You get slimes two ways: drop a primer item on a wild slime to prime it into a resource, or farm them from Slime Milk once a source is running.

You can only scoop a slime once it is down to its smallest size, so cut the big ones apart first. Right-click a small slime with an empty bucket to catch it in a Bucket of Slime, then carry it to a frog or a Slime Milker.

Each species spawns in country that suits its resources. Where to look:

Cave: dripstone and lush caves.
Geode: high mountain peaks, the stony, jagged, and frozen kind.
Bog: swamps and mangrove swamps.
Tide: oceans and open water.
Infernal: the Nether.
Void: the small outer islands of the End.

Slime Milk is a single fluid, but each bucket keeps track of which slime it came from, along with any catalyst buffs, held on the milk itself. That means it keeps its resource through pipes and tanks. It does not flow as a world fluid; it stays in buckets, tanks, and the source block you place.

Put a captured slime into a Slime Milker to draw off a bucket of its Slime Milk. Pour or pipe that milk into a placed Slime Milk source block, or a Slime Milk Basin, and it spawns Resource Slimes of the same resource, so many before it runs dry.

Four catalyst items tune a placed Slime Milk source. Drop one into the source, by tossing it in or piping it with a dropper, and the source consumes it and takes the upgrade. The buff is saved to that source and comes back with it if you bucket it up again. Catalysts are optional and on by default; a pack can switch them off in the config.

Bountiful Catalyst: adds more spawns before the source runs dry. There is no cap, so stack as many as you like.
Rapid Catalyst: shortens the time between spawns, up to a cap.
Teeming Catalyst: adds another slime to each spawn, up to a cap.
Endless Catalyst: the source never runs dry. It is built from Bountiful catalysts, the top of that line.

The Slime Milk Basin is the hands-off way to spawn slimes. Feed it Slime Milk and it spawns Resource Slimes of that resource, the same way and at the same rate a placed source block does. Point it at a pipe network and it runs on its own.

Pour a Slime Milk bucket in, or pipe milk from a tank, and the basin spawns that resource's slimes, catalysts and all. It is waterloggable and sits as a half-block, so it drops into a pipe network cleanly. A plain placed source block spawns the same way if you would rather not build one.
book.productivefrogs.guide.terrarium.con: The Controller is the anchor of the Terrarium and its only milk input. Pipe Slime Milk into it, or drop a bucket in its slot, and it feeds the Sprinklers overhead. Right-click it once the box is built and it checks the whole sealed shell: if everything is legal the block lights up, and if it isn't, it names the first thing wrong, like a missing shell block or a machine sitting on an edge.

The Controller holds one kind of milk at a time and refuses a second until it drains empty. That is on purpose. A Cave frog eats iron and copper, so run iron milk through to fill a few Sprinklers, let the buffer empty, then run copper for the rest. Each Sprinkler keeps whatever it was given, so both kinds rain side by side.
book.productivefrogs.guide.terrarium.spr: Sprinklers sit in the ceiling cells over the cavity, up to 25 of them, and turn the Controller's milk into slimes that rain down for the frogs to eat. Each one holds a bucket's worth and spawns exactly as a placed Slime Milk source would. Right-click a filled Sprinkler with an empty bucket to drain its milk back out.

A Sprinkler uses up its milk as it feeds, so the Terrarium keeps drawing on the milk you pipe in. Feed it milk carrying the Endless catalyst and it stops running down, the set-and-forget option once you can make Endless milk. Sprinklers also pause once the cavity is full of slimes, then pick back up as the frogs eat them down, and a redstone signal on a ceiling cell pauses that Sprinkler without spending any milk.
book.productivefrogs.guide.terrarium.inc: The Incubator is optional, and it carries frog stats into the Terrarium's frogs. Seed it from the outward face with a bottled Frog Egg for baseline stats, or a captured tadpole to keep the stats it already has. The frog matures inside and steps out into the cavity with those stats intact.

Breed two same-species frogs inside a formed Terrarium, clicking a Sweetslime onto each as you would anywhere, and their frogspawn goes straight into an Incubator instead of onto water, bred stats and all. More Incubators means a faster population ramp. At the frog cap of 8 a mature frog waits inside until a slot frees, and right-clicking an incubating one with a Sweetslime hurries it along.
book.productivefrogs.guide.terrarium.hat: Every Froglight the frogs produce inside a formed Terrarium drops straight into the Hatch inventory. Nothing spawns as a loose item to chase down or hopper up. Pull the Froglights out of its outward face by hand, or pipe them off.

Leave the Hatch full and the frogs stop eating until you clear it. Nothing drops on the floor and nothing is voided, the loop just goes quiet. Empty it, by hand or with a pipe, and the frogs get back to work.
